mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon, as investors anticipate improved earnings.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS shows consistent month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and consequently S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by >0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) are high, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank. This environment typically increases the cost of capital and reduces future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL > 3% and FEDFUNDS > 4% simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signifies expanding supply-side capacity and improving business conditions. This positive signal translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat during post-recession recovery periods despite INDPRO showing consistent month-over-month growth above 0.2%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand in the construction sector and broader economic slowdown. This reduction in investment and consumer confidence leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where HOUST declines by >10% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) indicates a strong and healthy labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues. This positive economic backdrop drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat despite PAYEMS showing consistent month-over-month growth above 0.1% for a sustained period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of low inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with low Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an accommodative monetary policy. This environment supports equity valuations by keeping discount rates low and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods where CPIAUCSL < 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 2% for a sustained period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth (GDPC1) signifies a strong overall economy, leading to higher corporate profits and increased investor confidence. This fundamental strength translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized.
